items.title: Whole energy cyber resilience requirements: reshaping cyber regulation in downstream gas and electricity
items.summary: We're seeking views on proposals for a new approach to cyber resilience regulation for downstream gas and electricity operators.

items.title: Outgoing BBC Director General to lead Creative Industries Council
items.summary: Tim Davie to succeed current industry co-chair Sir Peter Bazalgette from 1 August

items.title: UK Trade Tariff: duty suspensions and autonomous tariff quotas
items.summary: Temporary duty suspensions and autonomous tariff quotas (ATQs) for importing goods into the UK.

items.title: Notice: CCUS East Coast Cluster: Teesside selection process
items.summary: Apply to take part in the ECC Teesside selection process for projects seeking connection to the Teesside network by 2032.

items.title: Guidance: CHPQA guidance notes version control index 2026
items.summary: CHPQA guidance notes version control index 2026

items.title: Guidance: CHPQA unit list
items.summary: This CHP unit list provides 'specs' so small engine-based CHP schemes can check their performance against the design performance of the relevant unit.

items.title: Guidance: CHPQA generic scheme energy flow diagrams
items.summary: CHPQA generic scheme energy flow diagrams which may be used to support submissions for simple schemes.

items.title: Guidance: CHPQA appendix 4: Determination of Z-ratio by plant performance test
items.summary: Example 4 outlines determination of Z-ratio by plant performance test.

items.title: Guidance: CHPQA appendix 3: Determination of useful heat output by direct use of gas turbine exhaust gases
items.summary: Example considering a CHP Scheme with direct use of the exhaust from the gas turbine for a process drying duty.

items.title: Guidance: CHPQA guidance note 61: Reporting of fiscal benefits gained from CHPQA certification
items.summary: The annual information needed on the monetary or fiscal benefits received as a result of being certified under the Combined Heat and Power Quality Assurance (CHPQA) scheme.

items.title: Guidance: CHPQA guidance note 50: Quantifying and justifying useful heat outputs
items.summary: The supporting evidence needed to accompany Design Submissions for the quantification and justification of qualifying heat.

items.title: Guidance: CHPQA guidance note 44: Use of CHPQA in respect of the Renewables Obligation and Contracts for Difference
items.summary: Guidance on accessing support measures for the electrical output of renewable combined heat and power (CHP).

items.title: Guidance: CHPQA guidance note 43: Use of CHPQA to obtain exemption from business rating of CHP plant and machinery
items.summary: The eligibility of CHP schemes for exemption from business rating for plant and machinery (P&M), and step-by-step guidance on how to claim such exemption.

items.title: Guidance: CHPQA guidance note 41: Use of CHPQA to obtain exemption from climate change levy and relief from fuel duty
items.summary: Exemption from the climate change levy and relief from fuel duty, for some or all of fuel inputs and electricity outputs, are fiscal benefits available to good quality CHP schemes.

items.title: Guidance: CHPQA guidance note 31: CHP scheme less than or equal to 2 MW
items.summary: Guidance on achieving CHPQA certification for small combined heat and power schemes of less than or equivalent to 2MW.

items.title: Guidance: CHPQA guidance note 30: CHP serving community heating
items.summary: Outline of the Good Quality criteria for CHP schemes which serve Community Heating.

items.title: Guidance: CHPQA guidance note 29: Alternative fuels - energy inputs
items.summary: Outline of fuel energy inputs for alternative fuels.

items.title: Guidance: CHPQA guidance note 28: The determination of Z ratio
items.summary: Guidance on determining the trade-off between heat to site and power for CHP schemes - the Z ratio.

items.title: Guidance: CHPQA guidance note 27: Determination of CHP qualifying power capacity (QPC)
items.summary: Guidance on determining CHP QPC, the maximum power generating capacity of the CHP scheme that can be considered to be generated in Good Quality CHP.

items.title: Guidance: CHPQA guidance note 26: Determination of CHP qualifying power output
items.summary: Guidance on determining CHP qualifying power output for a scheme.

items.title: Guidance: CHPQA guidance note 25: Determination of CHP qualifying fuel input
items.summary: Guidance on how to determine  CHP qualifying fuel input.

items.title: Guidance: CHPQA guidance note 24: Determination of scheme quality
items.summary: Guidance on how the quality of a CHP scheme is determined.

items.title: Guidance: DBT worldwide offices
items.summary: Contact details for Department for Business and Trade (DBT) offices around the world and a list of our HM Trade Commissioners.

items.title: Accredited official statistics: Building materials and components statistics: March 2026
items.summary: Provides information on selected building materials and contains monthly data on price indices, bricks, cement and concrete blocks; and quarterly data on sand and gravel, slate, concrete roofing tiles and ready-mixed concrete.

items.title: Make Work Pay: threshold for triggering collective redundancy obligations
items.summary: We are seeking views on the level and methods by which the new organisation-wide threshold for triggering collective redundancy obligations might be set.

items.title: Draft Technology Transfer Agreements Block Exemption Order
items.summary: This technical consultation seeks feedback on the legal drafting of the Competition Act 1998 (Technology Transfer Agreements Block Exemption) Order 2026.

items.title: Policy paper: Smart Data Strategy
items.summary: This strategy sets out the long‑term vision for smart data in the UK, and the actions government will take to unlock growth, competition and innovation through secure, trusted data sharing across the economy.

items.title: Transparency data: DBT register of board members’ interests 2025 to 2026
items.summary: A register of the private interests declared by the Department for Business and Trade (DBT) board members.

items.title: Notice: Trade remedies notice: registration of imports of rutile titanium dioxide originating from China
items.summary: Trade remedies notice published by the Secretary of State for Business and Trade relating to the registration of imports of rutile titanium dioxide originating from China.

items.title: Government safeguards critical UK CO2 supplies with restart of plant
items.summary: The Government has shored up the UK’s critical supplies of CO2 vital for Britain’s nuclear, packaged meats, fresh food and healthcare by temporarily restarting the Ensus bioethanol plant in Wilton, Teesside.

items.title: Pan-Euro Mediterranean Convention on Rules of Origin (PEM)
items.summary: The Department for Business and Trade (DBT) is seeking views on the potential impacts of UK accession to the Regional Convention on Pan-Euro Mediterranean Preferential Rules of Origin (PEM).

items.title: Transparency data: DBT: spending over £500, January 2026
items.summary: Spending by electronic purchasing card solution (ePCS), over £500.

items.title: Transparency data: DBT: spending over £25,000, February 2026
items.summary: Reports on departmental spending over £25,000.

items.title: Transparency data: DBT: spending over £500, December 2025
items.summary: Spending by electronic purchasing card solution (ePCS), over £500.

items.title: Official Statistics: Trade and investment factsheets (partner names beginning with S)
items.summary: A snapshot of the UK’s trade and investment positions with individual trading and investment partners overseas, for partners whose name begins with S.

items.title: Official Statistics: Trade and investment factsheets (partner names beginning with M to O)
items.summary: A snapshot of the UK’s trade and investment positions with individual trading and investment partners overseas, for partners whose name begins with M, N or O.

items.title: Official Statistics: Trade and investment factsheets (partner names beginning with J to L)
items.summary: A snapshot of the UK’s trade and investment positions with individual trading and investment partners overseas, for partners whose name begins with J, K or L.

items.title: Transparency data: DBT: spending over £25,000, January 2026
items.summary: Reports on departmental spending over £25,000.

items.title: Official Statistics: Trade and investment factsheets (partner names beginning with A or B)
items.summary: A snapshot of the UK’s trade and investment positions with individual trading and investment partners overseas, for partners whose name begins with A or B.

items.title: Official Statistics: Trade and investment factsheets (partner names beginning with C to F)
items.summary: A snapshot of the UK’s trade and investment positions with individual trading and investment partners overseas, for partners whose name begins with C, D, E or F.
